origin.capability_match: Submit Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) for whitelisting

attack_surface.data_description: High volume of fake, low-quality, or spam RFRV submissions.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: Developer RFRV Submission to Berachain Foundation Review

security_gap.gap_description: The RFRV review process (Component 8) can be overwhelmed by spam, affecting its availability for legitimate submissions (Data Flow 57 availability_required: true).

actor_risk.description: Developer (or multiple attackers) abuses the RFRV submission process by submitting excessive spam.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Overwhelming the review process can delay or prevent legitimate projects from being whitelisted.

assumptions: There are no effective rate-limiting, anti-spam measures, or submission costs for the RFRV channels.
The review capacity of the Berachain Foundation (Component 8) is finite and can be exhausted by a flood of submissions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement CAPTCHAs, submission fees (potentially nominal or refundable for valid proposals), rate limiting on submission channels, and clear filtering criteria to quickly discard spam or low-effort RFRVs.

description: A developer (Actor 5) or multiple malicious actors flood the RFRV submission system (Behavior 110, Data Flow 57) with a large volume of fake or low-quality submissions, overwhelming the review capacity of the Berachain Foundation (Component 8) and causing a denial of service for legitimate submissions.

trace_source.matched_capability: Submit Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) for whitelisting
